title: haplophyllum suaveolens var. glabrum
intro: Haplophyllum suaveolens var. glabrum, also known as the small-flowered dodder-laurel, is a plant species that belongs to the Rutaceae family. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antioxidant activities. The health benefits of Haplophyllum suaveolens var. glabrum include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory issues, and treat digestive problems, as well as its potential to protect against certain diseases. Traditionally, the plant is used in herbal preparations such as infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are often employed to treat various health conditions and promote overall well-being.
